winner: I dropped a hammer on a flower... Now I have petunias all over my hands.

candidates.text: I dropped a hammer on a flower... Now I have petunias all over my hands.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke uses both required words in a creative and surprising way, with a play on 'petunias' (a type of flower) and the mess caused by dropping a hammer. It is concise and funny, though the humor could be slightly more refined."}

candidates.text: Why did the hammer bring a flower to school? Because it wanted to be a blossom builder!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses the required words and has a playful twist with 'blossom builder,' but the pun is somewhat forced and the humor is mild."}

candidates.text: Why did the hammer bring a flower to the party? Because it couldn't cling to any other topic!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses both required words but the humor is weak and relies on a forced pun with 'cling' that doesn't fully connect to 'hammer' or 'flower'."}

candidates.text: Why don't gardeners ever use a hammer on their roses? Because they prefer to petal to the metal!
Why don't hammers ever give flowers? Because they can't find the right nail-a-daisy!
